title: Documentary Requirements for Accreditation of Principal/Employer.
content: 38 The following documentary requirements shall be submitted to the Department, through 39 the Philippine LMA for accreditation of the principal/employer: 40 41 A. Letter request from the LMA; 42 43 B. Verified/authenticated/attested SPA issued by the principal/employer, which 44 includes explicit authorization for the LMA, among other provisions, to enter into 45 any and all contracts as its true and legal representative to act for and on its behalf 46 in the recruitment, hiring and placement of Filipino seafarers for overseas 47 employment; 48 49 C. Verified/authenticated/attested MA containing, among others, the responsibilities 50 of both principal/employer and manning agency with respect to the recruitment and 51 employment of seafarers; 52 53 D. Valid business license or registration certificate (in official English translation) of 54 the principal/employer or equivalent document or proof of existence of business 55 validated or certified by the issuing authority in the host country; 56 57 E. Board Resolution, Secretary’s Certificate, or letter from the principal/employer 58 granting authority to its employee or officer to act as its duly-authorized 59 representative or signatory in the MA and SPA; 60 61 31 F. List of ships together with the individual certificates of vessel registry (CVR) (in 1 official English translation) or an equivalent document. 2 3 In case of a newbuilding vessel, a certificate from the ship builder/owner that the 4 ship is under construction indicating its delivery date, and an affidavit of 5 undertaking of the LMA to submit the valid CVR; 6 7 G. Crew complement and corresponding wage scale; 8 9 H. Ship’s Insurance Certificate of Cover (COC) for existing ship and affidavit of 10 undertaking ensuring the continuing validity of the COC. For new building ship, 11 affidavit of undertaking of the agency to submit the COC upon ship’s delivery and 12 its continuing validity; 13 14 I. Financial security document/s compliant with MLC, 2006, as amended, and an 15 affidavit of undertaking ensuring the continuing validity of the financial security, 16 subject to the provisions of R.A. No. 12021 and its IRR. 17 18 For vessels not covered by the financial security document, an Affidavit of 19 Undertaking on the repatriation and compensation of the crew in the event of 20 abandonment; and 21 22 J. Ship Management Agreement, Concessionaire Agreement, Bareboat Charter 23 Agreement, Crew Management Agreement, or any other equivalent certificate 24 affirming that the principal/employer has an agreement with the shipowner. 25 26 Any changes or amendments to the above list of requirements shall be subject to 27 issuance of necessary guidelines by the Department upon stakeholder consultations. 28 29
